question: Will Russia gain more territory than it loses in June 2026? Resolution criteria: Resolves to the outcome of the question found at https://manifold.markets/a_l_e_x/will-russia-gain-more-territory-tha-pPnUl9s9Ig. Background: Resolution criteria This market will resolve YES if, according to a majority of the resolution sources, Russia achieves a net territorial gain in June 2026. This market will resolve NO if, according to a majority of the resolution sources, Russia does not achieve a net territorial gain in June 2026. Resolution sources The resolution sources are: Black Bird Group AMK Mapping Divgen Close date This market is set to close at 23:59 UTC on 7 July 2026, although it may resolve sooner. Clarifications This market will be voided if two or more resolution sources fail to provide an estimate before the close date.
event_title:
Will Russia gain more territory than it loses in June 2026?

Resolution criteria: Resolves to the outcome of the question found at https://manifold.markets/a_l_e_x/will-russia-gain-more-territory-tha-pPnUl9s9Ig.

Background: Resolution criteria

This market will resolve YES if, according to a majority of the resolution sources, Russia achieves a net territorial gain in June 2026.

This market will resolve NO if, according to a majority of the resolution sources, Russia does not achieve a net territorial gain in June 2026.

Resolution sources

The resolution sources are:

Black Bird Group

AMK Mapping

Divgen

Close date

This market is set to close at 23:59 UTC on 7 July 2026, although it may resolve sooner.

Clarifications

This market will be voided if two or more resolution sources fail to provide an estimate before the close date.

However, in its May 2, 2026, Russian offensive campaign assessment, ISW warns that “comparisons of Russian gains this year with those of previous years when infiltration tactics were rarer thus are not straightforward, as Russian forces exert a significantly lower degree of control over infiltration areas than they do over seized areas.” This complicates inferences drawn from raw annual comparisons of changes in territorial control. 7. evidences.content: | In the past week (June 2–9, 2026): | Russian forces saw a net gain of 6 square miles of Ukrainian territory, following a 10 square mile loss during the previous week of May 26–June 2, 2026. | Russian forces saw a net loss of 10 square miles of Ukrainian territory, following a 14 square mile loss during the previous week of May 26–June 2, 2026. evidences.title: Mapping Russian attacks and territorial gains across Ukraine
evidences.content: Yes, keep me updated Moscow’s forces concentrated fighting around the logistical hub of Kostiantynivka, which they entered in December, in a bid to gain control of Kramatorsk and Sloviansk – the last major cities under Ukrainian control in Donetsk. In December, the ISW estimated Russian advances had claimed no more than 5 percent of Kostiantynivka. In the north, Ukraine managed to hold off Russia’s yearlong campaign for Kupiansk in the Kharkiv region with a surprise counteroffensive in December. The map below shows four years of territorial changes on the ground, highlighting Russia’s gains and Ukraine’s efforts to regain territory. Image 3: INTERACTIVE - UKRAINE - GAINS - FEB 23, 2026 copy-1771917424 (Al Jazeera) ## Air and drone attacks have increased threefold

evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 20, 2026 | ISW
evidences.content: Ukrainian forces continue targeting Russian artillery and drone operators in east (left) bank Kherson Oblast. The commander of a Ukrainian artillery battery reported on June 20 that Russian forces are continuing to shell the west (right) bank Kherson Oblast but had to move their artillery systems deeper in the rear due to the constant threat from Ukrainian artillery and first-person view (FPV) strikes. The commander noted that Russian forces are carefully camouflaging their towed guns near Oleshkivsky Pisky (around 30 kilometers east of Kherson City) and that Ukrainian forces are targeting Russian drone operators. The commander implied that Russian towed guns and drone operators hiding in dense urban areas are harder for Ukrainian forces to detect and disable. The commander added that [...] Map Thumbnail Map Thumbnail Ukrainian and Russian forces continue targeting each other’s frontline drone positions in the Kostyantynivka-Druzhkivka tactical area. The Ukrainian General Staff reported on June 20 that Ukrainian forces struck a Russian drone control point near occupied Soledar, Donetsk Oblast (northeast of Kostyantynivka) on June 19. The Russian MoD published footage on June 20...

evidences.title: Ukraine war situation update | 6 – 12 June 2026
evidences.content: During the week of 6 to 12 June, Russian forces attacked at least 14 petrol stations in Ukraine’s front line regions, in a trend that had been intensifying since April. On 9 June alone, Russian drones damaged three different petrol stations in the villages of Korobochkyne, Malynivka, and Pechenihy in the Kharkiv region. In the Chernihiv region, Russian drones attacked petrol stations in Koriukivka and Novhorod-Siversky. They also attacked the cities of Nikopol and Pavlohrad and the villages of Bohdanivka and Hrushivka in the Dnipropetrovsk region, as well as a petrol station in the city of Kherson. In some of these strikes, Russian forces launched small drones at targets 20 to 25 kilometers within the front line. They mostly caused fires to erupt, without inflicting mass casualties. This [...] ReliefWeb logo ## Content Search Ukraine # Ukraine war situation update | 6 – 12 June 2026 Ukraine Conflict Monitor Update Russia steps up its targeting of critical civilian infrastructure along Ukraine’s front line regions. 1,937 political violence events 3% increase compared to last week 205 incidents of violence targeting civilians 6% decrease compared to last week At least 61 civilians...

evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 13, 2026 | ISW
evidences.content: Russian forces conducted a series of long-range drone strikes against Ukraine on the night of June 12 to 13. The Ukrainian Air Force reported that Russian forces launched 118 Shahed-type, Gerbera-type, and Italmas-type strike drones, Banderol-type loitering munitions, and Parodiya-type decoy drones from the directions of Kursk and Oryol cities; Millerovo, Rostov Oblast; Primorsko-Akhtarsk, Krasnodar Krai; occupied Donetsk City, and occupied Hvardiiske, Crimea. The Ukrainian Air Force reported that Ukrainian forces downed 110 drones, that three drones struck three locations, and that debris fell on six locations. Ukrainian officials reported that Russian forces struck civilian, residential, and commercial infrastructure in Dnipropetrovsk, Mykolaiv, and Sumy oblasts. [...] Ukrainian forces continue their intermediate-range strike campaign against Russian military assets in occupied Donetsk Oblast on the night of June 12 to 13 and previously on June 5. The Ukrainian General Staff reported that Ukrainian forces struck a Russian command post and a manpower concentration near occupied Soledar (roughly22 kilometers from the frontline) and a manpower concentration near occupied Uspenivk...

evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 19, 2026 | ISW
evidences.content: Russian forces continued limited ground operations in western Zaporizhia Oblast on June 18 and 19 but did not make confirmed advances. A Russian milblogger claimed on June 18 that Russian forces recently advanced beyond Mala Tokmachka (southeast of Orikhiv), but the spokesperson for a Ukrainian brigade operating in the area reported on June 19 that Russian forces do not even hold positions within Mala Tokmachka and advanced at closest 300 meters from the settlement before Ukrainian forces repelled the Russian forces. A prominent Kremlin-affiliated Russian milblogger posted a map on June 19 acknowledging that the Russian Forward Line of Own Troops (FLOT) is further west and south of Nesteryanka (southeast of Zaporizhzhia City) than ISW previously assessed, though these Ukrainian gains are [...] ISW previously assessed, though these Ukrainian gains are not within the last 24 hours. [...] 6. Ukraine’s intermediate- and long-range strike campaign against Russian fuel infrastructure continues to strain gasoline supplies. Russian authorities continue to impose fuel rationing in Russia and occupied southern Ukraine while some authorities deny that Russia is facing fuel supply shortages...

Russian forces consistently deploy Kh-101, Iskander-M, Iskander-K, and Shahed/Geran drones to strike Ukraine and will likely use these technological adaptations to increase the scale and scope of the damage such strikes inflict. evidences.title: AMK Mapping (Telegram channel) — Grokipedia
evidences.content: AMK Mapping provides real-time reports on Russian missile and drone strikes targeting Ukrainian infrastructure and cities, often detailing launch types, trajectories, and impacts within specific oblasts. These updates include summaries of attack scales alongside assessments of resulting damage. The channel tracks airstrikes and troop movements, highlighting Russian Su-57 operations in contested areas like Kursk Oblast, while incorporating OSINT-derived geolocations to map incursion points and defensive responses. Maps visualize frontline shifts and strike zones, emphasizing Ukrainian advances or Russian setbacks in event selections that align with a pro-Ukraine viewpoint.(#ref-5)](#ref-5) Coverage extends to Ukrainian offensive actions, such as tactical aircraft deploying Storm Shadow [...] AMK Mapping provides real-time updates on Middle East conflicts, including airstrikes, ballistic missile launches, and troop movements in hotspots such as Israel, Iran, Lebanon, and the West Bank.(#ref-6)](#ref-6)(#ref-7)](#ref-7)(#ref-8)](#ref-8) This coverage integrates OSINT-sourced imagery and geolocation data to track events like Israeli operations in southern Lebanon and Iranian strikes...

evidences.title: Arms Trends in Ukraine: 1 June - 7 June, 2026
evidences.content: Ukrainian analysts at Defense Express note that one of the key factors determining whether the system can intercept ballistic targets such as the Iskander-M will be the FP-7.x's ability to perform intensive maneuvers. For effective and reliable interception of ballistic missiles, it is not enough for an air-defence missile to simply explode nearby. It must accurately strike the warhead directly, using the so-called “hit-to-kill” principle. Such a high level of accuracy must be achieved even when the enemy ballistic missile is actively maneuvering at speeds of several Mach. [...] Meanwhile, actual deliveries of Patriot interceptors to Ukraine are already complicated by the fact that the United States wants to replenish its own stockpiles after the war with Iran, during which it reportedly used 45% to 61% of its Patriot interceptor stocks. Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, the UAE, and Saudi Arabia have similar concerns, as they also reportedly fired several thousand Patriot interceptors. According to the analysts, interceptor missiles alone cannot be the answer to Russia’s ballistic missile threat. Without strikes on launchers, missile stockpiles, and production facilities, no missile-defe...

evidences.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, June 20, 2026 | ISW
evidences.content: Map Thumbnail [...] Map Thumbnail Russian forces continued infiltrations in the Kupyansk direction. Geolocated footage published on June 20 shows two Russian servicemembers infiltrating into central Radkivka (north of Kupyansk). Russian milbloggers claimed on June 20 that Russian forces advanced into Radkivka and northeast and west of Novovasylivka (northeast of Kupyansk). Map Thumbnail [...] Russian forces continue infiltration missions deeper into Kostyantynivka, indicating that Russian forces are likely consolidating at least some positions in the city while interspersed with Ukrainian positions. ISW has updated its control of terrain assessment in Kostyantynivka by expanding Russian infiltration areas and Russian advances in the city. ISW increasingly has observed evidence of Russian forces infiltrating further into northern, northwestern, and western Kostyantynivka in recent days and has not observed evidence of Ukrainian forces maintaining an organized defense in the easternmost and southernmost sections of Kostyantynivka, indicating that Russian forces have likely advanced in southern Kostyantynivka and up to a segment of the northern outskirts of Kostyantynivka.
